channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Academy announces major overhaul to rules
item.description: The new rules focus on areas such as AI protections for writers and actors and expanded eligibility for international films.
item.content:encoded:
The new rules focus on areas such as AI protections for writers and actors and expanded eligibility for international films.

(Image credit: Frederic J. Brown)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: Banksy confirms new statue installed in central London is his work
item.description: The statue in Waterloo Place, which appeared early Wednesday, depicts a man proudly hoisting a flag— but the flag is blinding him.
item.content:encoded:
The statue in Waterloo Place, which appeared early Wednesday, depicts a man proudly hoisting a flag— but the flag is blinding him.

(Image credit: Kin Cheung)
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas

item.title: 'Wait Wait' for May 2, 2026: Live in San Diego with Ron Burgundy
item.description: This week, we're live in San Diego with host Peter Sagal, judge and scorekeeper Bill Kurtis, special guest Ron Burgundy, and panelists Eugene Cordero, Paula Poundstone, and Alzo Slade
item.content:encoded:
This week, we're live in San Diego with host Peter Sagal, judge and scorekeeper Bill Kurtis, special guest Ron Burgundy, and panelists Eugene Cordero, Paula Poundstone, and Alzo Slade

(Image credit: Evan Agostini)

item.title: On "SANGÚ," Arturo Sandoval reaches back and pays it forward
item.description: For more than 60 years, this maestro of magic has collaborated with towering figures. Now on a new record, he turns to family.
item.content:encoded:
For more than 60 years, this maestro of magic has collaborated with towering figures. Now on a new record, he turns to family.

(Image credit: Joseph Gray)
item.dc:creator: Ryan Benk

item.title: New Netflix documentary reexamines Winnie Mandela's divisive legacy
item.description: Winnie Madikizela-Mandela is one of the most revered — and controversial — women in South African history. In a new documentary her granddaughters examine the liberation icon in all her complexity.
item.content:encoded: Winnie Madikizela-Mandela is one of the most revered — and controversial — women in South African history. In a new documentary her granddaughters examine the liberation icon in all her complexity.
item.dc:creator: Kate Bartlett

item.title: 'The Devil Wears Prada' showed us how trends spread. 20 years later, what's changed?
item.description: In 2006, an infamous scene from The Devil Wears Prada schooled viewers on how fashion trends make their way from the runway to the clearance bin. 20 years later, what's changed?
item.content:encoded:
In 2006, an infamous scene from The Devil Wears Prada schooled viewers on how fashion trends make their way from the runway to the clearance bin. 20 years later, what's changed?

(Image credit: Scott A Garfitt)
item.dc:creator: Ayana Archie

item.title: Here's what musician Amy Grant likes about getting older
item.description: On Wild Card, well-known guests answer the kinds of questions we often think about but don't talk about. Musician Amy Grant talks about what she likes about getting older.
item.content:encoded: On Wild Card, well-known guests answer the kinds of questions we often think about but don't talk about. Musician Amy Grant talks about what she likes about getting older.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Martin

item.title: 'The Devil Wears Prada 2' is a fizzy sequel -- with more to say than the original
item.description: Like any good sequel, this movie feels like a reunion. Anne Hathaway, Emily Blunt and Meryl Streep return in a cleverly written film that will delight anyone who loved the 2006 original.
item.content:encoded: Like any good sequel, this movie feels like a reunion. Anne Hathaway, Emily Blunt and Meryl Streep return in a cleverly written film that will delight anyone who loved the 2006 original.
item.dc:creator: John Powers

item.title: Kneecap is used to controversy. On new album 'Fenian,' they're leaning further in
item.description: NPR's Juana Summers talks with Mo Chara and Móglaí Bap of the Irish hip-hop trio Kneecap about their new album Fenian.
item.content:encoded:
NPR's Juana Summers talks with Mo Chara and Móglaí Bap of the Irish hip-hop trio Kneecap about their new album Fenian.

(Image credit: Yamil Lage/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Kathryn Fink

item.title: This 'Devil Wears Prada' wants to save journalism
item.description: Andy Sachs returns to Runway in a timely but unconvincing story about saving the magazine.
item.content:encoded:
Andy Sachs returns to Runway in a timely but unconvincing story about saving the magazine.

(Image credit: Macall Polay)
item.dc:creator: Linda Holmes
